sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Gossamer Bio Investors File Securities Lawsuit Over Alleged Misstatements About Phase 3 Drug Trial
feed.summary: A federal securities lawsuit has been filed against Gossamer Bio, Inc. and its CEO, alleging misleading statements about its Phase 3 PROSERA study for the drug seralutinib. The lawsuit claims that Gossamer concealed known risks related to patient enrollment and an unusual placebo response at Latin American clinical sites, which ultimately led to the drug trial failing to meet its primary endpoint and caused the company's stock to plummet over 80%. Investors who purchased Gossamer securities during the class period of June 16, 2025, through February 20, 2026, and incurred losses may have been directly affected by the alleged misconduct.

feed.title: Synopsys Partners with TSMC to Power Next-Generation AI Systems with Silicon Proven IP and Certified EDA Flows
feed.summary: Synopsys announced significant advancements in silicon-proven IP, AI-powered EDA flows, and system-level enablement in collaboration with TSMC for next-generation AI and high-performance computing designs. This partnership focuses on enhancing 3DFabric technologies, accelerating design productivity with AI-assisted verification, and expanding their IP portfolio for advanced, specialty, and automotive TSMC nodes. These efforts aim to improve quality of results, accelerate development cycles, and push boundaries in performance, integration, and energy efficiency.

feed.title: Bank of America signals shift in software sector as AI reshapes outlook
feed.summary: Bank of America has updated its outlook on the infrastructure software sector, upgrading Twilio to "Buy" with a price target of $190 due to its strong position in AI-driven voice and messaging infrastructure. Conversely, GitLab was downgraded to "Neutral" with its price target cut to $27 due to slowing revenue growth, increased competition from AI startups, and execution challenges. The bank highlights that AI is rapidly transforming competitive dynamics and investor expectations within the software sector, requiring companies to demonstrate accelerating revenue growth and improved profitability to justify premium valuations.

feed.title: Greene County Bancorp Maintains Quarterly Dividend, MHC Waives Payout
feed.summary: Greene County Bancorp announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.10 per share, maintaining its previous rate. Its majority owner, Greene County Bancorp, MHC, will waive its right to this dividend, benefiting public shareholders and allowing the company to preserve capital. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates GCBC as "Outperform" due to strong financials, attractive valuation, and positive technicals.

feed.title: Cadence and TSMC widen AI chip tools from 3nm to A14
feed.summary: Cadence and TSMC are expanding their collaboration to accelerate AI-driven semiconductor innovation, focusing on advanced process technologies from N3 to A14. This partnership aims to provide end-to-end design infrastructure and certified flows to help customers reduce design iterations and accelerate time to market for AI and HPC designs. The initiative also emphasizes "agent-ready" design flows that integrate agentic AI to optimize power, performance, area (PPA) and reliability.

feed.title: Erie Indemnity (ERIE) director adjusts deferred compensation share credits
feed.summary: Erie Indemnity director C. Scott Hartz reported an update to his holdings, including 1,097.427 indirect Class A common shares and an adjustment of 110.609 Directors' Deferred Compensation Share Credits at $253.85 per credit. These credits, now totaling 19,309.235, represent rights to Class A shares upon the termination of his board service, with no set exercise or expiration dates. The transaction, classified as neutral in sentiment, reflects routine dividend reinvestment under the company's deferred compensation plans.

feed.title: Company (NYSE: STT) files Rule 144 notice for restricted shares
feed.summary: State Street Corp (NYSE: STT) has filed a Rule 144 notice indicating an intent to sell restricted common stock. The filing details multiple vesting events with specific share lots and dates ranging from November 2023 to May 2025, with Fidelity Brokerage Services LLC listed as the intermediary. This regulatory filing signals that previously restricted shares are becoming eligible for resale to the public.

feed.title: Erie Indemnity (ERIE) director updates deferred share credits and holdings
feed.summary: Erie Indemnity Company director Thomas B. Hagen has reported updated equity holdings and deferred-share credits. The filing details an acquisition of 82.838 Directors' Deferred Compensation Share Credits through dividend reinvestment, bringing his total deferred account to 14,461.094 share credits tied to Class A common stock. Hagen also maintains large indirect positions in Class A and Class B shares through family trusts and a limited partnership, while disclaiming beneficial ownership beyond his pecuniary interest.

feed.title: Greene County Bancorp approves quarterly cash dividend of $0.10 per share
feed.summary: Greene County Bancorp has approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.10 per share, maintaining an annual rate of $0.40. The dividend's record date is May 15, 2026, with an expected payment date of May 29, 2026. The majority stockholder, Greene County Bancorp, MHC, will waive its receipt of this dividend.

feed.title: Why Super Micro Computer Shares Are Climbing On Wednesday?
feed.summary: Super Micro Computer Inc (NASDAQ: SMCI) shares are trading higher on Wednesday due to a broader market relief rally, particularly in the tech sector, and a decline in short interest. The company also recently launched new compact edge AI systems using AMD EPYC 4005 processors. Despite the recent gains, SMCI is still working to recover its longer-term trend from a significant drawdown.

feed.title: Fair Isaac stock hits 52-week low at $908.00 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Fair Isaac (FICO) stock has hit a 52-week low of $905.80, reflecting significant annual declines despite impressive gross profit margins and revenue growth. The downturn is primarily attributed to increased competition, particularly from Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac's adoption of VantageScore 4.0, and regulatory pressures concerning FICO's pricing strategies. While several analysts maintain positive ratings, price targets have been adjusted due to market volatility and competitive risks.

feed.title: Wells Fargo lent to MFS as Barclays pulled out - report (WFC:NYSE)
feed.summary: Wells Fargo provided a loan to a property portfolio associated with Market Financial Solutions (MFS). This happened shortly after Barclays began freezing MFS accounts due to the property lender being on the verge of collapse. The report highlights Wells Fargo's involvement following Barclays' withdrawal.

feed.title: Stock selection is critical amid high volatility with Fed and midterms: Raymond James' Larry Adam
feed.summary: The article highlights the importance of careful stock selection in a volatile market environment. It emphasizes that this volatility is driven by factors such as the Federal Reserve's actions and the upcoming midterm elections, according to Larry Adam of Raymond James.

feed.title: Masco jumps 12% on earnings beat, remodeling demand improves
feed.summary: Masco Corporation saw its stock jump 12.6% after reporting strong first-quarter earnings that surpassed analyst expectations, driven by pricing actions and cost savings. The company also reaffirmed its full-year 2026 guidance and noted improving demand signals in the home remodeling market, despite a challenging housing backdrop. Structural factors like high home equity and an aging housing stock are cited as underlying support for repair and remodel activity.

feed.title: First Guaranty Bancshares Appoints Betsy Hood to Board
feed.summary: First Guaranty Bancshares (FGBI) has appointed Betsy K. Hood to its Board of Directors, bringing over 25 years of experience in the retail automotive industry. Hood's extensive community leadership and regional ties are expected to strengthen the bank's local connections and community-focused profile. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates FGBI as Neutral due to concerns about profitability and cash flow, mixed valuation, and soft technical indicators, despite some positive corporate events.

feed.title: People, Companies & Products
feed.summary: This article summarizes recent personnel changes, company acquisitions, and new product offerings within the drilling and energy sectors. Key moves include Samir Ali becoming CEO of Seadrill, Halliburton acquiring Sekal, and Liz Westcott being named CEO of Woodside Energy, alongside new technologies from companies like Halliburton and Expro aiming to enhance drilling efficiency and safety.

feed.title: Erie Indemnity (NASDAQ: ERIE) director updates stock, trust and deferred share holdings
feed.summary: Erie Indemnity director Jonathan Hirt Hagen reported updated holdings, primarily reflecting routine plan-related activity. The update included the addition of 97.345 Directors' Deferred Compensation share credits through dividend reinvestment, bringing his total to 16,993.818 share credits. The filing also detailed direct ownership of Class A and Class B shares, significant indirect Class B positions held through trusts, and minor indirect Class A positions where beneficial ownership is disclaimed.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Ameriprise Financial (AMP) After Its Strong Multi‑Year Run?
feed.summary: Ameriprise Financial (AMP) has seen significant multi-year returns but logged a slight decline recently. Analysis suggests AMP is undervalued according to both an Excess Returns model, indicating a 38.2% undervaluation, and its P/E ratio of 11.74x which is below industry averages and Simply Wall St's Fair Ratio of 14.01x. The article encourages investors to consider these valuation metrics.

feed.title: IBM Earnings Today: Red Hat, Confluent and AI Risk Put a $15.6 Billion Quarter in Focus
feed.summary: International Business Machines Corp. is preparing to report its first-quarter results, with analysts keenly focused on software performance and the impact of the recently completed $11 billion Confluent acquisition on its 2026 guidance. While adjusted earnings are expected to be around $1.81 per share on $15.6 billion in revenue, investors are particularly interested in the growth sustainability of IBM's software division and Red Hat's hybrid-cloud software. The quick Confluent integration is fueling expectations for a stronger growth outlook, despite mixed analyst price target adjustments and lingering concerns about the consulting arm’s performance.

feed.title: Freeport-McMoRan Inc Stock (FCX) Moved Up by 3.69% on Apr 22: Drivers Behind the Movement
feed.summary: Freeport-McMoRan Inc (FCX) stock rose by 3.69% on April 22, driven by anticipation for its Q1 2026 earnings report, a bullish outlook for copper and gold prices, and positive analyst revisions. The impending earnings, strong commodity market dynamics, the restart of the Grasberg mine, and a recently declared dividend are fueling investor confidence, despite some intraday volatility. The stock's performance reflects positive sentiment ahead of key financial announcements and a favorable commodity environment.

feed.title: A Look At Enphase Energy (ENPH) Valuation As Grid Integration And Commercial Expansion Advance
feed.summary: Enphase Energy (ENPH) is expanding its commercial microinverter shipments and integrating its IQ Batteries with virtual power plant platforms, despite a weak short-term performance for investors. Analysts predict revenue recovery by 2027, with a widely followed narrative suggesting a fair value of $76.86 per share, significantly above its current trading price of $33.62. This valuation largely depends on long-term revenue growth, firm margins, and the company's ability to capitalize on the solar market recovery.

feed.title: Fifth Third Bank evaluating Dallas real estate, decision expected in 'short order'
feed.summary: Fifth Third Bank is evaluating real estate options in Dallas for its North Texas division, aiming for "HQ2" status after acquiring Comerica. While a decision is expected soon, the bank has committed to maintaining a prominent real estate footprint in Dallas, including considering downtown, Uptown, and Preston Center. Its current lease at Comerica Bank Tower ends in 2028, and the bank plans significant expansion in Texas, aiming for 250 financial centers by 2029.

feed.title: Betsy Hood joins First Guaranty Bancshares (NASDAQ: FGBI) board of directors
feed.summary: First Guaranty Bancshares, Inc. announced the election of Betsy K. Hood to its Board of Directors on April 16, 2026. Ms. Hood brings over 25 years of experience in the retail automotive industry and holds positions as Dealer Principal of Hood Automotive Group and President of Southern Dealers Mechanical Breakdown Insurance. She has not yet been appointed to any board committees and is not involved in any related-party transactions requiring disclosure.

feed.title: NVR (NYSE: NVR) Q1 2026 earnings: net income down 34% on softer margins
feed.summary: NVR, Inc. reported a significant decline in its first-quarter 2026 financial results, with net income falling 34% to $198.4 million and consolidated revenues decreasing 22% to $1.88 billion. The downturn was attributed to softer homebuilding margins, impacted by pricing pressure and higher lot costs, despite an increase in new orders. Mortgage banking income also saw a decline, reflecting a decrease in closed loan production.

feed.title: EOG Maintained by Scotiabank -- Price Target Raised to $139
feed.summary: Scotiabank analyst Betty Zhang has raised EOG Resources Inc.'s price target from $123.00 to $139.00 while maintaining a Sector Perform rating. According to GuruFocus, EOG Resources is currently considered 3.9% overvalued with a GF Value™ of $127.46, despite a strong GF Score™ of 79/100 indicating solid fundamentals. Insider activity shows $2.5 million in shares sold over the past three months, with no reported purchases.

feed.title: USA Today ranks Carrier a climate leader for cutting emissions intensity
feed.summary: Carrier Global Corporation (NYSE: CARR) has been recognized by USA Today as one of America's Climate Leaders for the third consecutive year. The ranking, developed with Statista, assesses U.S.-headquartered companies on their reductions in emissions intensity relative to revenue. Carrier's sustained progress in reducing greenhouse gas emissions and improving operational efficiency across its solutions led to this recognition.

feed.title: MGM Resorts completes sale of MGM Northfield Park operations
feed.summary: MGM Resorts International has finalized the sale of its MGM Northfield Park operations in Ohio to private equity funds managed by Clairvest Group for $546 million in cash, with expected net proceeds of $420 million after taxes. The sale is part of MGM's strategy to divest non-strategic regional assets at a higher multiple, enabling them to maintain a strong balance sheet, invest in growth, and return capital to shareholders. The master lease agreement with VICI Properties has been adjusted, reducing MGM's annual rent by $53 million.

feed.title: Xcel Energy Plans Power Shutoffs Impacting Thousands Amid Elevated Fire Danger
feed.summary: Xcel Energy is implementing Public Safety Power Shutoffs across western and southern Colorado due to high wildfire risks, affecting approximately 14,000 customers, especially in the San Luis Valley. These precautionary shutoffs are in response to strong winds, dry vegetation, and low humidity, which increase the potential for electrical equipment to spark wildfires. Customers are advised to prepare for outages and stay informed as conditions evolve.

feed.title: Sherwin-Williams declares $0.80 quarterly dividend
feed.summary: The Sherwin-Williams Company (NYSE:SHW) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.80 per common share, payable on June 5, 2026, to shareholders recorded by May 22, 2026. The paint giant boasts 33 consecutive years of dividend increases and an annual yield of 0.96%. While InvestingPro suggests the stock is currently overvalued, analysts have offered varied price targets ranging from $355 to $420.

feed.title: FedEx cuts more than 100 jobs at two facilities
feed.summary: FedEx has cut 106 jobs across two facilities in New York and Pennsylvania. One of these job reductions is connected to the company's Network 2.0 initiative, as reported by the Memphis Business Journal.

feed.title: Sherwin-Williams Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.80 a Share, Payable June 5 to Shareholders of Record as of May 22
feed.summary: Sherwin-Williams (NYSE: SHW) announced it will maintain its quarterly dividend at $0.80 per common share. The dividend is scheduled to be paid on June 5, 2026, to shareholders of record as of May 22, 2026. This announcement comes amidst recent analyst rating adjustments and price target changes for the company.

feed.title: Twilio upgraded by Bank of America on AI-driven growth outlook
feed.summary: Bank of America has upgraded Twilio Inc. (NYSE:TWLO) to 'Buy' from 'Underperform', increasing its price objective to $190 from $110. This upgrade is based on improving fundamentals and a strong growth outlook driven by AI, with Twilio being seen as a crucial infrastructure layer for emerging AI applications, especially in voice and messaging. The bank anticipates accelerating gross profit growth and expanding free cash flow margins, further supported by upcoming quarterly earnings and the annual Signal customer event.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs (GSCE) issues callable 3‑underlier notes; capped $1,236 call payment
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs Finance Corp. is offering structured, non-interest bearing callable notes linked to the Russell 2000® Index, EURO STOXX 50® Index, and iShares® MSCI Emerging Markets ETF, maturing on April 25, 2029. These notes feature an automatic call option on April 20, 2027, paying $1,236 per $1,000 face amount if all underliers are at or above their initial levels. If not called, the maturity payout is determined by the lesser performing underlier, offering 300% upside participation for positive returns but significant principal loss potential if the lesser performing underlier falls below 80% of its initial level.

feed.title: [S-3] CINCINNATI FINANCIAL CORP Shelf Registration Statement
feed.summary: Cincinnati Financial Corporation has filed an S-3 Shelf Registration Statement for 500,000 shares of common stock under its Shareholder Investment Plan. This plan allows new and existing investors to purchase shares directly and reinvest dividends, with minimum reinvestment requirements and optional cash investments. The prospectus details enrollment, purchase mechanics, fees, shareholder rights, and tax considerations, highlighting the company's insurance business and potential risks associated with stock investment.

feed.title: MGM Resorts Sells Northfield Park Venue for $546M
feed.summary: MGM Resorts International has sold its operations at MGM Northfield Park for $546 million to Clairvest Group Inc., as part of MGM's strategy to focus on higher-growth assets like destinations and digital offerings. Despite the venue's profitability and popularity, MGM CEO Bill Hornbuckle stated it no longer fits their core vision. Clairvest, a company known for investing in regional casinos, gains a stable and profitable venue, with net proceeds for MGM estimated at $420 million after taxes and expenses.

feed.title: Proxy advisor ISS urges ConocoPhillips shareholders to vote for independent board chair
feed.summary: Proxy advisor Institutional Shareholder Services (ISS) has recommended that ConocoPhillips shareholders vote for a proposal advocating an independent board chair, citing the need for stronger oversight of management. The non-binding proposal seeks to separate the roles of chair and CEO, with an independent director at the helm of the board, to provide more robust independent oversight and address concerns about the current lead director role and ConocoPhillips' underperformance relative to the S&P 500. ConocoPhillips' board, which currently has Ryan Lance serving as both chairman and CEO, has recommended against the proposal, stating that the combined role with an independent lead director is in shareholders' best interests.

feed.title: Templeton Emerging Markets Fund Announces $0.0475 Monthly Distribution Plan for 2025
feed.summary: Templeton Emerging Markets Income Fund (NYSE: TEI) has announced a monthly distribution of $0.0475 per share for 2025, payable on January 31, 2025, to shareholders of record on January 21, 2025. This managed distribution plan aims to provide consistent monthly payouts and potentially narrow the discount between the fund's market price and its net asset value. The fund intends to generate these distributions primarily from net investment income, but may use long-term capital gains or return of capital if necessary.

feed.title: Viking Holdings Ltd Cash Flow – GETTEX:ZG8
feed.summary: This article provides key cash flow data for Viking Holdings Ltd (ZG8) as of Q4 2025. It highlights a free cash flow of 574.16 million EUR for Q4 2025, with an annual free cash flow of 1.11 billion EUR and operating cash flow of 1.98 billion EUR for the same year. The information is presented through various financial statements focusing on operating, investing, and financing activities.

feed.title: Firefly Aerospace (FLY) slides 4.6% as investors refocus on potential share overhang after recent resale filing
feed.summary: Firefly Aerospace (FLY) experienced a 4.6% drop in its stock price, largely attributed to investor concerns over a potential share overhang following a recent resale filing and updated financing disclosures. The company filed a prospectus for the resale of over 11 million shares and also updated its credit facility terms, which may have led to reassessments of its balance sheet flexibility. This decline could also be a result of routine profit-taking after a recent strong rally or the expiration of a stock lock-up in February 2026.

feed.title: Supreme Court rules in favor of Army veteran wounded in suicide attack
feed.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled in favor of an Army veteran injured in a 2016 suicide bombing in Afghanistan, allowing him to sue a military contractor, Fluor Corporation. The 6-3 decision determined that the contractor was not immune from liability because it allegedly failed to supervise the attacker, an employee, who built a bomb on base. This ruling reverses lower court decisions and may pave the way for similar lawsuits against war-zone contractors for actions outside their authorized duties.
feed.source: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
feed.source_domain: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Teledyne Technologies beats Q1 2026 forecasts
feed.summary: Teledyne Technologies reported strong financial results for Q1 2026, exceeding EPS and revenue forecasts, which led to a 4.75% rise in pre-market stock trading. The company's performance was significantly driven by growth in the defense and space markets, leading them to raise their full-year 2026 guidance for sales and non-GAAP EPS. Teledyne's CEO highlighted strong operational momentum and strategic positioning in key markets, committing to capacity expansion to meet unprecedented demand.

feed.title: Skyworks Solutions (SWKS) Stock Is Up, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Skyworks Solutions (SWKS) shares rose after Barclays upgraded its rating to Overweight and increased its price target, citing expectations that Apple will delay the launch of lower-end phones, which could benefit Skyworks as an Apple supplier. The stock's jump follows a recent drop due to escalating trade tensions between the US and China and geopolitical concerns impacting the semiconductor supply chain. Despite the recent uptick, SWKS is down year-to-date and significantly below its 52-week high, with long-term investors seeing a substantial loss.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase, Wells Fargo funnel $1.95M into west Charlotte economic push
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase and Wells Fargo Foundations have committed a combined $1.95 million to support economic mobility and housing stability initiatives in west Charlotte, focusing on the Historic West Side. A key project is the conversion of a decommissioned school into an Economic Mobility Hub, offering cooperative manufacturing, workforce training, and entrepreneurship opportunities. JPMorgan Chase alone has invested $700,000 for planning and financial modeling of this hub, as part of its broader strategy to foster inclusive wealth-building in historic neighborhoods.

feed.title: Why Halliburton (HAL) Stock Is Up Today
feed.summary: Halliburton (HAL) stock rose 3.3% after reporting better-than-expected first-quarter 2026 financial results and benefiting from rising oil prices. The company's revenue and profit surpassed analyst estimates, driven by share repurchases and improved profitability. Halliburton's stock is up 32.1% year-to-date and is trading near its 52-week high.

feed.title: Textron To Expand European Parts and Distribution Center in Düsseldorf
feed.summary: Textron Aviation is set to expand its European parts and distribution center in Düsseldorf. This expansion will add 1,000 square meters of space and increase the parts inventory by 5,000 items to enhance fulfillment services for its customers. The move aims to improve support and efficiency for Textron Aviation operations in Europe.

feed.title: Elong Power (ELPW) surges as low-float trading ramps up after recent filings and listing actions
feed.summary: Elong Power (ELPW) saw a significant surge of 56.9% in its stock price, attributed to technical and speculative momentum in a low-float stock. This movement follows recent corporate actions including a 1-for-80 share consolidation, the filing of its annual report, and a transfer of its listing to the Nasdaq Capital Market. Hedge fund activity shows a mix of decreases and additions to ELPW holdings from Q4 2025 to Q1 2026.

feed.title: Sherwin-Williams declares $0.80 quarterly dividend By Investing.com
feed.summary: The Sherwin-Williams Company announced a quarterly dividend of $0.80 per common share, payable on June 5, 2026, to shareholders of record as of May 22, 2026. This marks the 33rd consecutive year the paint and coatings giant has raised its dividend. Several analysts have updated their price targets for Sherwin-Williams, with RBC Capital, UBS, and Morgan Stanley maintaining positive ratings, while Argus lowered its price target but still holds a Buy rating.

feed.title: Fair Isaac (FICO) slides as mortgage-score pricing comes under renewed scrutiny
feed.summary: Fair Isaac (FICO) stock dropped 6.7% due to renewed investor concerns about political and industry pressure on its mortgage-credit-score pricing, potentially curbing future pricing power and increasing competitive adoption of alternative models. A U.S. Senate oversight letter highlighted a planned price increase for FICO's mortgage-related per-score price in 2026, raising regulatory and policy response risks. Despite solid recent results and reiterated full-year guidance, the market is re-rating the stock due to these pressures.

feed.title: How is eBay Essential to the Circular Economy and Advancing Environmental Stewardship?
feed.summary: eBay plays a crucial role in advancing the circular economy and environmental stewardship through its recommerce platform, which encourages reusing and reselling products. In 2025, eBay's platform generated $5.3 billion in positive economic impact while avoiding 1.6 million metric tons of carbon emissions and diverting 71,000 metric tons of waste. The company also supports circularity through its Circular Fashion Fund and has a Climate Transition Plan aiming for net-zero emissions by 2045.

feed.title: Bristol Myers Squibb Cutting 206 More Jobs
feed.summary: Bristol Myers Squibb announced an additional 206 job cuts between July and December 2026 as part of an ongoing cost-cutting strategy aiming for $2 billion in savings by 2027. These layoffs contribute to more than 1,700 positions eliminated since January 2025, driven by efforts to streamline operations and enhance efficiency. The company seeks to become a "leaner, more efficient company" while investing in growth brands and promising scientific areas.
